note: Australian-produced. Grounded in the course's own modules. Founder to review before wiring live.

quizzes.title: Improving Indoor Air Quality After Water Damage — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: According to the course, what is the most common contaminant found in a building after water damage?
questions.sourceRef: M1 — The Impact of Water Damage on Indoor Air Quality

questions.questionText: How soon can mould start growing after water exposure?
questions.sourceRef: M2 — Identifying Common Contaminants After Water Damage

questions.questionText: Which tool is used to measure the concentration of volatile organic compounds (VOCs) in the air in real time?
questions.sourceRef: M3 — Tools and Techniques for Assessing Indoor Air Quality

questions.questionText: Which device is used to detect biological contamination on surfaces by measuring adenosine triphosphate?
questions.sourceRef: M2 — Identifying Common Contaminants After Water Damage

questions.questionText: To help prevent further mould growth, relative humidity should be kept below what level?
questions.sourceRef: M3 — Tools and Techniques for Assessing Indoor Air Quality

questions.questionText: Which non-invasive tool identifies temperature differences in surfaces that often correlate with hidden moisture?
questions.sourceRef: M3 — Tools and Techniques for Assessing Indoor Air Quality

questions.questionText: During mould remediation, what is the purpose of a negative air pressure system?
options: To contain mould and prevent spores from migrating to other areas of the building
To raise indoor humidity to speed up drying
To measure the concentration of VOCs in the air
To record before-and-after air quality readings
questions.sourceRef: M4 — Remediation Techniques for Improving Indoor Air Quality

questions.questionText: What should documentation of air quality improvements include after remediation is complete?
options: Before-and-after air quality measurements such as moisture, air sampling, VOC and humidity readings
Only a verbal description with no measurements recorded
Just the final invoice provided to the client
A prediction of the building's future resale value
questions.sourceRef: M5 — Documenting and Reporting Air Quality Improvements
